description: Pipeline-style anomaly detection toolkit for time series data with statistical, contextual, and collective anomaly detection methods
key_features: Contextual anomaly detection based on time series patterns
Collective anomaly detection using machine learning

usage_examples.title: Basic Statistical Outlier Detection
usage_examples.description: Detect statistical outliers using z-score method
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv('time_series_data.csv')

# Create pipeline and detect outliers
anomalies = (
    AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | detect_statistical_outliers(
        columns=['value'],
        method='zscore',
        threshold=3.0
    )
)

# Get results
results = anomalies.data

usage_examples.title: Contextual Anomaly Detection
usage_examples.description: Detect anomalies based on seasonal patterns and trends
usage_examples.code:
# Detect contextual anomalies with seasonal adjustment
contextual_anomalies = (
    AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | detect_contextual_anomalies(
        columns=['sales'],
        time_column='date',
        method='residual',
        model_type='sarimax',
        seasonal_period=12
    )
)

results = contextual_anomalies.data
usage_examples.use_case: Sales anomaly detection with seasonal patterns

usage_examples.title: Ensemble Anomaly Detection
usage_examples.description: Combine multiple detection methods for robust results
usage_examples.use_case: Comprehensive anomaly detection for critical systems

parameters.group_columns: Columns to group by for panel data

main_functions.name: detect_contextual_anomalies
main_functions.description: Detect anomalies by comparing with expected values

main_functions.name: detect_collective_anomalies

description: Pipeline-style cohort analysis tool for customer behavior and retention analysis

usage_examples.title: Customer Retention Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze customer retention by signup month
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_retention

# Load customer data
df = pd.read_csv('customer_data.csv')

# Create cohorts and calculate retention
retention_analysis = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | form_time_cohorts(
        date_column='signup_date',
        time_period='M'
    )
    | calculate_retention(
        cohort_column='cohort',
        date_column='activity_date', 
        user_id_column='user_id',
        max_periods=12
    )
)

# Get retention matrix
retention_matrix = retention_analysis.retention_matrices

usage_examples.title: Behavioral Cohort Analysis
usage_examples.description: Segment users by behavior patterns
usage_examples.code:
# Create behavioral cohorts based on spending
behavioral_cohorts = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | form_behavioral_cohorts(
        behavior_column='total_spent',
        conditions={
            'high_value': ('>', 1000),
            'medium_value': ('between', 100, 1000),
            'low_value': ('<=', 100)
        }
    )
    | calculate_lifetime_value(
        cohort_column='cohort',
        date_column='purchase_date',
        user_id_column='user_id',
        value_column='revenue'
    )
)

main_functions.name: form_time_cohorts
main_functions.description: Form cohorts based on time periods

parameters.format_cohorts: Whether to format as readable strings

description: User funnel analysis using cohort framework for conversion optimization

usage_examples.title: Basic Funnel Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze conversion through signup funnel
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from funnelanalysis import analyze_funnel
from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe

# Load event data
df = pd.read_csv('user_events.csv')

# Analyze funnel
funnel_result = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | analyze_funnel(
        event_column='event_name',
        user_id_column='user_id',
        funnel_steps=['landing', 'signup', 'activation', 'purchase'],
        step_names=['Landing', 'Sign Up', 'Activate', 'Purchase']
    )
)

# Get funnel data
funnel_data = funnel_result.conversion_funnels['overall_funnel']

usage_examples.title: Funnel Analysis by Time Period
usage_examples.description: Track funnel performance over time
usage_examples.code:
# Analyze funnel trends over time
time_funnel = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | analyze_funnel_by_time(
        event_column='event_name',
        user_id_column='user_id', 
        date_column='event_date',
        funnel_steps=['view', 'cart', 'checkout', 'purchase'],
        time_period='M'
    )
)

monthly_funnel = time_funnel.conversion_funnels['time_funnel']

parameters.funnel_steps: List of events in funnel order

main_functions.name: analyze_user_paths
main_functions.description: Analyze actual user paths through funnel

description: Pipeline-style metrics analysis tool for calculating business and statistical metrics

usage_examples.title: Basic Metric Calculations
usage_examples.description: Calculate summary statistics for business metrics
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from metrics_tools import MetricsPipe, Count, Mean, Sum, Ratio

# Load sales data
df = pd.read_csv('sales_data.csv')

# Calculate metrics
metrics = (
    MetricsPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | Count('orders', 'total_orders')
    | Sum('revenue', 'total_revenue') 
    | Mean('order_value', 'avg_order_value')
    | Ratio('revenue', 'orders', 'revenue_per_order')
).Execute()

print(metrics)

usage_examples.title: Statistical Analysis
usage_examples.description: Perform statistical analysis on metrics
usage_examples.code:
# Calculate advanced statistics
stats_analysis = (
    MetricsPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | Variance('revenue')
    | StandardDeviation('revenue')
    | Correlation('revenue', 'marketing_spend')
    | Percentile('revenue', 95)
).Execute()

print(stats_analysis)

usage_examples.title: Pivot Table Analysis
usage_examples.description: Create pivot tables for multi-dimensional analysis
usage_examples.code:
# Create pivot table by region and product
pivot_result = (
    MetricsPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | PivotTable(
        index='region',
        columns='product_category', 
        values='revenue',
        aggfunc='sum'
    )
).ShowPivot()

print(pivot_result)

description: Pipeline-style moving aggregation toolkit for time series analysis
key_features: Simple and exponentially weighted moving averages
Moving sums, counts, and custom aggregations

usage_examples.title: Stock Price Analysis
usage_examples.description: Calculate moving averages for stock prices
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from movingaverages import MovingAggrPipe, moving_average, moving_variance

# Load stock data
df = pd.read_csv('stock_data.csv')

# Calculate moving metrics
result = (
    MovingAggrPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | moving_average(
        columns=['close_price'],
        window=20,
        method='simple'
    )
    | moving_average(
        columns=['close_price'], 
        window=50,
        method='exponential',
        output_suffix='_ema'
    )
    | moving_variance(
        columns=['close_price'],
        window=20
    )
)

results_df = result.data

usage_examples.title: Sales Trend Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze sales trends with moving statistics
usage_examples.code:
# Analyze sales trends with grouped data
sales_analysis = (
    MovingAggrPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | moving_average(
        columns=['daily_sales'],
        window=7,
        method='simple',
        time_column='date',
        group_columns=['store_id']
    )
    | moving_zscore(
        columns=['daily_sales'],
        window=30,
        group_columns=['store_id']
    )
)

main_functions.name: moving_variance
main_functions.description: Calculate moving variance and std dev

main_functions.name: moving_correlation
main_functions.description: Calculate moving correlations between column pairs

parameters.column_pairs: List of column pairs to correlate

description: Pipeline-style experimental analysis tool for A/B testing and causal inference

usage_examples.title: A/B Test Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze experiment results with statistical rigor
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, PercentChange, BootstrapCI

# Load experiment data
df = pd.read_csv('experiment_data.csv')

# Analyze treatment effects
results = (
    OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | PercentChange(
        condition_column='treatment_group',
        baseline='control'
    )
    | BootstrapCI(
        condition_column='treatment_group',
        baseline='control',
        metric_column='conversion_rate',
        n_bootstraps=10000
    )
).ShowOperation()

print(results)

usage_examples.title: CUPED Analysis
usage_examples.description: Reduce variance using pre-experiment covariates
usage_examples.code:
# Apply CUPED for variance reduction
cuped_results = (
    OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | CUPED(
        condition_column='treatment',
        baseline='control',
        covariates=['pre_experiment_metric'],
        stratified_by=['user_segment']
    )
).ShowOperation()

print(cuped_results)

description: Pipeline-style risk analysis toolkit for maximum likelihood estimation and portfolio risk
key_features: Value at Risk (VaR) and CVaR calculations

usage_examples.title: Portfolio Risk Analysis
usage_examples.description: Calculate comprehensive risk metrics for investment portfolio
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from riskanalysis import RiskPipe, fit_distribution, calculate_var, calculate_portfolio_risk

# Load returns data
df = pd.read_csv('portfolio_returns.csv')

# Comprehensive risk analysis
risk_analysis = (
    RiskPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | fit_distribution(
        columns=['stock_returns'],
        distribution='student_t'
    )
    | calculate_var(
        columns=['stock_returns'],
        confidence_level=0.05,
        method='parametric'
    )
    | calculate_portfolio_risk(
        columns=['stock_a', 'stock_b', 'stock_c'],
        weights=[0.4, 0.4, 0.2]
    )
)

risk_metrics = risk_analysis.risk_metrics

usage_examples.title: Stress Testing
usage_examples.description: Perform stress tests under adverse scenarios

description: Pipeline-style customer segmentation tool using machine learning clustering
key_features: K-means clustering with optimal K selection

usage_examples.title: Customer Segmentation
usage_examples.description: Segment customers using behavioral data
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from segmentationtools import SegmentationPipe, get_features, run_kmeans, generate_summary

# Load customer data
df = pd.read_csv('customer_data.csv')

# Perform customer segmentation
segmentation = (
    SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | get_features(
        columns=['recency', 'frequency', 'monetary'],
        scale_features=True
    )
    | run_kmeans(
        n_clusters=5,
        find_optimal=True
    )
    | generate_summary()
)

print(segmentation)

description: Pipeline-style time series operations for lead/lag analysis and statistical testing

usage_examples.title: Economic Time Series Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze economic indicators with leads and lags
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from timeseriesanalysis import TimeSeriesPipe, lag, lead, variance_analysis

# Load economic data
df = pd.read_csv('economic_data.csv')

# Time series analysis
analysis = (
    TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | lag(
        columns=['gdp_growth'],
        periods=4,
        time_column='quarter'
    )
    | lead(
        columns=['unemployment'],
        periods=2,
        time_column='quarter'
    )
    | variance_analysis(
        columns=['inflation'],
        method='rolling',
        window=8
    )
)

results = analysis.data

usage_examples.title: Distribution Analysis
usage_examples.description: Analyze distributions of time series data
usage_examples.code:
# Analyze return distributions
dist_analysis = (
    TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | distribution_analysis(
        columns=['returns'],
        bins=20,
        group_columns=['asset_class']
    )
    | cumulative_distribution(
        columns=['returns'],
        time_column='date'
    )
)

summary = dist_analysis | get_distribution_summary()

description: Pipeline-style trend analysis tool for time series decomposition and forecasting

usage_examples.title: Sales Trend Analysis
usage_examples.description: Comprehensive analysis of sales trends and seasonality
usage_examples.code:
import pandas as pd
from trendanalysistools import TrendPipe, aggregate_by_time, decompose_trend, forecast_metric

# Load sales data
df = pd.read_csv('sales_data.csv')

# Comprehensive trend analysis
trend_analysis = (
    TrendPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | aggregate_by_time(
        date_column='sale_date',
        metric_columns=['revenue', 'units_sold'],
        time_period='M'
    )
    | calculate_growth_rates(
        window=12,
        annualize=True
    )
    | decompose_trend(
        metric_column='revenue',
        model='multiplicative',
        period=12
    )
    | forecast_metric(
        metric_column='revenue',
        periods=6,
        method='holt_winters'
    )
)

# Get results
growth_data = trend_analysis.trend_results
forecasts = trend_analysis.forecasts

usage_examples.title: Statistical Trend Testing
usage_examples.description: Test for statistically significant trends
usage_examples.code:
# Test for significant trends
trend_test = (
    TrendPipe.from_dataframe(df)
    | aggregate_by_time(
        date_column='date',
        metric_columns=['website_traffic'],
        time_period='W'
    )
    | calculate_statistical_trend(
        metric_column='website_traffic',
        test_method='mann_kendall',
        alpha=0.05
    )
    | compare_periods(
        metric_column='website_traffic',
        comparison_type='year_over_year'
    )
)
